meta.title: Draft protocol / ingredient additions (the 'add on demand' lane)
meta.purpose: New protocols and ingredients are staged here as DRAFTs and merged into the chairside menu with a DRAFT badge, so the 3-auditor-locked iv-module.json stays clean until the Medical Director signs and promotes them. This is the mechanism for adding protocols over time per PROTOCOL-AUTHORING-FORMAT.md.
meta.published_by: Armando A. Falcon, MD — AllyOS curator (editorial; clinic prescriber signs the actual standing order)

ingredients.name: Niagen / Nicotinamide Riboside (NR)
ingredients.class: NAD+ precursor (vitamin B3 family) — NOT a peptide, NOT IV NAD+
ingredients.guardrail: PUBLISHED AllyOS reference. To USE clinically the clinic's own prescriber must review and sign an NR standing order for their patients — AllyOS does not prescribe. NAD+ PRECURSOR — a DISTINCT molecule from infused NAD+ (ING_NAD); do NOT treat as interchangeable, and there is NO proven peptide synergy (direct_combination_evidence:false). Avoid in pregnancy/lactation; screen active cancer (theoretical, VERIFY). Sourcing/inventory is the clinic/provider's decision — AllyOS is pharmacy-agnostic. Evidence grade C-D (limited).

monograph.what: Nicotinamide riboside (Niagen® is a branded NR) is an NAD+ precursor in the vitamin B3 family that raises intracellular NAD+. It is NOT the same molecule as infused NAD+, and it is NOT a peptide.
monograph.relevance_nad: Cross-listed under NAD+: it elevates NAD+ through the precursor (salvage) pathway — an alternative/adjunct to direct IV NAD+, generally with a gentler infusion profile than NAD+ itself.
monograph.relevance_nonpeptide: Cross-listed under non-peptide wellness: providers often weigh it alongside peptide longevity protocols, but it is a small-molecule vitamin derivative, not a peptide — and there is no proven NR + peptide synergy.
monograph.evidence_note: Small retrospective (n=8 NR) + a non-peer-reviewed preprint for the IV route; oral NR is better studied. Moderate confidence for short-term tolerability, low for outcomes.
citations: Conze et al. Scientific Reports 2019 — oral NR (background)
Hawkins et al. medRxiv 2024 — single 500 mg NR IV (preprint)
Reyna et al. Frontiers in Aging 2026 (DOI 10.3389/fragi.2026.1652582; PMC12907335) — n=14, 500 mg NAD+/NR IV ×4 days

stacks.title: Niagen / NR (NAD+ precursor)
stacks.indication: NAD+ precursor wellness support via nicotinamide riboside — an alternative/adjunct to IV NAD+ with a gentler infusion. Distinct from IV NAD+; not a peptide.
stacks.scope_limitation: Adjunctive nutritional wellness support. Not intended to diagnose, treat, cure, prevent, reverse, or manage any disease. Adults >= 18. NOT IV NAD+; no proven peptide synergy.
key_gates: Prescriber-signed NR standing order REQUIRED (clinic's own prescriber)
Active cancer: screen / defer to oncology (VERIFY — limited data)
citations: Hawkins medRxiv 2024 (single 500 mg NR IV, preprint)
Reyna Front Aging 2026 (n=14, 500 mg NAD+/NR IV ×4 days)
stacks.note: Published AllyOS template. To use, the clinic's prescriber reviews, customizes, and signs the NR standing order for their patients (AllyOS does not prescribe). Evidence grade C-D (limited). See protocols/niagen-nr-iv-standing-order.md.
